title: Data entry administrators
description: Administer, test, and implement computer databases, applying knowledge of database management systems. Coordinate changes to computer databases. Identify, investigate, and resolve database performance issues, database capacity, and database scalability. May plan, coordinate, and implement security measures to safeguard computer databases. Design strategies for enterprise databases, data warehouse systems, and multidimensional networks. Set standards for database operations, programming, query processes, and security. Model, design, and construct large relational databases or data warehouses. Create and optimize data models for warehouse infrastructure and workflow. Integrate new systems with existing warehouse structure and refine system performance and functionality. Design, model, or implement corporate data warehousing activities. Program and configure warehouses of database information and provide support to warehouse users. (Based on 22 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Compile, sort, and verify the accuracy of data before it is entered. Locate and correct data entry errors, or report them to supervisors. Compare data with source documents, or re-enter data in verification format to detect errors. Store completed documents in appropriate locations. Write and code logical and physical database descriptions and specify identifiers of database to management system, or direct others in coding descriptions. Select and enter codes to monitor database performance and to create production databases. Review procedures in database management system manuals to make changes to database. Develop data models describing data elements and how they are used, following procedures and using pen, template, or computer software. Modify existing databases and database management systems or direct programmers and analysts to make changes. Test programs or databases, correct errors, and make necessary modifications. Develop standards and guidelines for the use and acquisition of software and to protect vulnerable information. Plan and install upgrades of database management system software to enhance database performance. Document, design, code, or test Geographic Information Systems (GIS) models, internet mapping solutions, or other applications. Create, analyze, report, convert, or transfer data, using specialized applications program software. Confer with users to analyze, configure, or troubleshoot applications. Provide technical support to junior staff or clients. Train users and answer questions. Keep records of work performed. Prepare and issue work schedules, deadlines, and duty assignments for office or administrative staff.

Operate and resupply printers and computers, changing print wheels or fluid cartridges, adding paper, and loading blank tapes, cards, or disks into equipment. Use data entry devices, such as optical scanners, to input data into computers for revision or editing. Transmit work electronically to other locations.

Maintain logs of activities and completed work. Check completed work for spelling, grammar, punctuation, and format. Electronically sort and compile text and numerical data, retrieving, updating, and merging documents as required. Collate pages of reports and other documents. Prepare reports, manuals, correspondence, or other information required by management or governmental agencies. Research, compile, and prepare reports to provide information regarding activities such as production or payroll. Analyze financial activities as input to budget planning processes.

Train or instruct employees in job duties or company policies or arrange for training to be provided. Evaluate employees' job performance and conformance to regulations and recommend appropriate personnel action. Review records pertaining to activities to verify details, monitor work activities, or evaluate performance. Discuss job performance problems with employees to identify causes and issues and to work on resolving problems.

Implement corporate or departmental policies, procedures, and service standards in conjunction with management. Review project requests describing database user needs to estimate time and cost required to accomplish projects. Make recommendations to management concerning staffing decisions or procedural changes. Consult with managers or other personnel to resolve problems in areas such as equipment performance, output quality, or work schedules.
Manage schedules and set dates, times, and locations for meetings and appointments.

Revise company definition of data as defined in data dictionary. Identify and evaluate industry trends in database systems to serve as a source of information and advice for upper management. Prepare training materials for, or make presentations to, users. Keep abreast of developments in document management technologies and techniques by reviewing current literature, talking with colleagues, participating in educational programs, attending meetings or workshops, or participating in professional organizations or conferences.

Perform other clerical duties, such as answering telephone, sorting and distributing mail, running errands or sending faxes. Adjust settings for format, page layout, line spacing, and other style requirements. Reformat documents, moving paragraphs or columns. Address envelopes or prepare envelope labels, using typewriter or computer. Retrieve specific sets of stored, typed characters to make changes. Print and make copies of work. File and store completed documents on computer hard drive or disk, or maintain a computer filing system to store, retrieve, update, and delete documents.
Remove data entry tasks related to medical records and other unrelated fields as they are irrelevant to data entry administrators.
Communicate with staff or clients to understand specific system requirements, and gather, analyze, or document user feedback to locate or resolve sources of problems.

Develop data models describing data elements and their use, and write and code logical and physical database descriptions, specifying identifiers of databases to management systems or directing others in coding descriptions.

Develop methods for integrating different products so they work properly together, including customizing commercial databases to fit specific needs, and collaborate with engineers or software developers to select appropriate design solutions or ensure the compatibility of system components.

Establish and calculate optimum values for database parameters, verify stability, interoperability, portability, security, or scalability of system architecture, and identify, evaluate and recommend hardware or software technologies to achieve desired database performance.
Test programs or databases, correct errors, make necessary modifications, and test backup or recovery plans regularly and resolve any problems.
Train users and answer questions, and train system users in system operation or maintenance.
Work as part of a project team to coordinate database development and determine project scope and limitations, and develop or approve project plans, schedules, or budgets.
Review existing or incoming data for currency, accuracy, usefulness, quality, or completeness of documentation, and monitor system operation to detect potential problems.
Set up or maintain monitoring tools on Web servers or Web sites, track, compile, and analyze Web site usage data and evaluate or recommend server hardware or software.
Document installation or configuration procedures to allow maintenance and repetition, and document and communicate database schemas, using accepted notations.
Identify or implement solutions to information security problems, and develop or install software, such as firewalls and data encryption programs, to protect sensitive information.

Define and analyze objectives, scope, issues, or organizational impact of information systems, and evaluate existing systems to determine effectiveness, suggesting changes to meet organizational requirements.

Perform ongoing hardware and software maintenance operations, including installing or upgrading hardware or software, and monitor Web developments through continuing education, reading, or participation in professional conferences, workshops, or groups.

Assist in the assessment, acquisition, or deployment of new electronic document management systems, and assist in determining document management policies to facilitate efficient, legal, and secure access to electronic content.
Develop Web site performance metrics and evaluate testing routines or procedures for adequacy, sufficiency, and effectiveness.

Conduct needs assessments to identify document management requirements of departments or end users, and assist in the development of document or content classification taxonomies to facilitate information capture, search, and retrieval.

Collaborate with Web developers to create and operate internal and external Web sites or to manage projects, such as e-marketing campaigns, and recommend Web site improvements, developing budgets to support recommendations.

Oversee development of plans to safeguard computer files against accidental or unauthorized modification, destruction, or disclosure, or to meet emergency data processing needs, and oversee performance of risk assessment or execution of system tests to ensure the functioning of data processing activities or security measures.
Investigate system component suitability for specified purposes and make recommendations regarding component use.
Develop information security standards and best practices, including training staff on, and overseeing the use of, information security standards, policies, and best practices.

Perform security analyses of developed or packaged software components, including testing issues such as system integration, performance, and system security on regular schedules or after any major program modifications.
Design and conduct hardware or software tests, develop testing routines and procedures, and correct testing-identified problems or recommend actions for their resolution.
Monitor systems for intrusions or denial of service attacks, and report security breaches to appropriate personnel.
Implement security measures for websites and databases, such as firewalls or message encryption.
Identify and correct deviations from database development standards.
Provide training or technical assistance in website implementation or usage.
Develop or maintain archived procedures, procedural codes, or queries for applications.
Confer with project personnel to identify and resolve problems related to database management.
Schedule and facilitate meetings related to information technology projects.
Monitor or track project milestones and deliverables in database projects.
Negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain necessary resources or materials for database projects.
Initiate, review, or approve modifications to project plans as needed for database implementation.
Identify, review, or select vendors or consultants to meet project needs for database development.
Establish and execute a project communication plan to ensure stakeholder engagement in database projects.
Identify need for initial or supplemental project resources to support database initiatives.
Direct or coordinate activities of project personnel in database-related projects.
Develop implementation plans that include financial analysis for database projects.
Coordinate the recruitment or selection of project personnel for database initiatives.
Develop and manage budgets for information technology projects, including database management initiatives.
Assign duties, responsibilities, and spans of authority to project personnel involved in database projects.
Prepare project status reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing information and trends related to database management.
Manage project execution to ensure adherence to budget, schedule, and scope in database implementation.
Develop or update project plans for information technology projects including objectives and resource specifications for database management.
Develop load-balancing processes to ensure optimal performance for database systems.
Monitor regulatory activity to maintain compliance with records and document management laws affecting databases.
Write, review, or execute plans for testing new or established document management systems, including related databases.
Search electronic sources, such as databases or repositories, for information.
Retrieve electronic assets from repository for distribution to users, and manage their return if necessary.
Propose recommendations for improving database and content management system capabilities.
Prepare support documentation and training materials for end users of databases.
Exercise security surveillance over document processing and database management to ensure data integrity.
Implement scanning or other automated data entry procedures using imaging devices and software.
Document technical functions and specifications for new or proposed database systems.
Develop, document, or maintain best practices or system usage procedures for databases.
Consult with end users regarding problems in accessing electronic content within databases.
Assess customer needs and priorities by communicating with them to understand requirements for databases.
Develop and manage work breakdown structure (WBS) for database-related information technology projects.
Monitor the performance of project team members, providing feedback related to database management tasks.
Write new programs or modify existing programs to meet customer requirements for database applications.
Submit project deliverables for database projects, ensuring adherence to quality standards.
Develop data models for applications, metadata tables, views, or related database structures.
Design databases to support various business applications, ensuring system scalability, security, and performance.
Design database applications, such as interfaces and data transfer mechanisms, for efficient access of data.
Demonstrate database technical functionality, such as performance, security, and reliability to stakeholders.
Create and enforce database development standards to ensure quality and consistency.
Collaborate with system architects and analysts to ensure database designs meet business requirements.
Develop database architectural strategies at the modeling, design, and implementation stages to address needs.
Develop and document database architectures that align with organizational goals.
Test software systems or applications related to database management for enhancements or new features.
Review designs, codes, test plans, or documentation to ensure database quality and functionality.
Provide or coordinate troubleshooting support for data warehouses and associated databases.
Prepare functional or technical documentation for data warehouses.
Develop data warehouse process models, including sourcing, loading, transformation, and extraction of data.
Verify the structure, accuracy, or quality of warehouse data and databases.
Develop or maintain standards for the design of data warehouse elements, including databases.
Select methods, techniques, or criteria for evaluating data warehousing processes and databases.
Perform risk assessments to develop strategies for database and data warehouse management.
Provide advice on project costs, design concepts, or design changes related to database initiatives.
Create or implement metadata processes and frameworks for data management.
Create plans, test files, and scripts for comprehensive testing of data warehouse systems.
Create supporting documentation for databases and data warehouses, including metadata diagrams and entity relationships.
Design and implement database structures for efficient data management.
Design, implement, or operate comprehensive data warehouse systems based on customer requirements.
Develop and implement data extraction procedures from various systems, ensuring data integrity.
Implement business rules through stored procedures and middleware technologies for databases.
Map data between source systems, data warehouses, and data marts for accuracy and efficiency.
Perform system analysis, data analysis or programming, using various computer languages and procedures for database management.
